titlestmt.titleproper: Gail and Stephen Rudin Civil War collection4696
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-08-17 00:09:00 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Gail and Stephen Rudin Civil War collection

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The Gail and Stephen Rudin collection of Civil War letters contains letters from Dr. William Harrison Githens: twenty-three letters to his wife, Sarah P. Robbins, one letter to his daughter Louise, two letters to an unnamed daughter, and one letter to his granddaughter Sarah. Dr. Githens mentions studying medicine in Philadelphia, various battles and the conditions for the soldiers, descriptions of battle wounds, church services, concerns about his family and other matters relating to "this cruel war" from Hamburg and Chattanooga, Tenn.; Philadelphia, Penn.; and near Rossville, near Marietta, near the Chattahoochee River, near Atlanta, in Atlanta, Ga.; Holly Springs, N.C.; Washington, D.C.; and Hamilton and Carthage, Ill. There is also an obituary of Dr. Githens from the Carthage [Illinois] Republican, July 13, 1904. Also included are 119 letters and other pieces from Civil War soldiers, officers, a wife in a camp, a musician in a band, a chaplain, a nurse, and at least one civilian to relatives, wives, friends, officers, pastor, and sweethearts re preparations for war, homesickness, battles, President Lincoln reviewing the troops, executions, and the surrender of General Lee. The writers of these letters were from much of the United States: Alabama; Arkansas; Washington, D.C.; Delaware; Georgia; Illinois; Kentucky; Louisiana; Maryland; Mississippi; Missouri; New York; North Carolina; Ohio; Tennessee; Texas; and Virginia.

label.ref: Series I. Letters from various authors
Series II. Letters from Dr. William Harrison Githens

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Collection contains some photocopies of items not owned by Cornell, these may not be reproduced for publication.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Gail and Stephen Rudin collection of Civil War letters, #4696. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: United States -- History -- Civil War, 1861-1865 -- Medical care.
United States -- History -- Civil War, 1861-1865 -- Casualties.
controlaccess.subject: Medicine -- Study and teaching -- United States.

did.unittitle: Series I. Letters from various authors
Writer: C.F. Noething Writer's location: Belleville, ILWriter's Military Unit: Unknown Addressee: Brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses Sexual Prowess (with three other family letters - no date, 8/31/61, 4/12/62).Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Walter Writer's location: Nashville, TNWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, UnknownAddressee: Father

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A week after Ft Sumter, the South prepares for war.Number of pages: 2

did.unittitle: Writer: Unsigned Writer's location: Pinckneyville, ILWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Sister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Passionate patriotic letter - "Never no Never" (Partial letter).Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Charles Ritner (Musician in Band) Writer's location: Washington Arsenal GroundsWriter's Military Unit: Union, Pennsylvania VolunteersAddressee: (Blank)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Document - Discharge for severe homesickness.Number of pages: 1

did.unittitle: Writer: Elmora Anthony Writer's location: Camp Trousdale, TNWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 7th Tennessee InfantryAddressee: Mother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wife has chosen to live with husband in camp.Number of pages: 5

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. John G. Darling Writer's location: Washington, DCWriter's Military Unit: Union, 2nd New Hampshire VolunteersAddressee: Friend Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes Washington D.C. and speaks of his negro beauty.Number of pages: 4. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Wells C. Haynes Writer's location: Fairfax Court House, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 2nd New Hampshire InfantryAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter written on captured colorized bookplate.Number of pages: 2. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Thomas M.C. Hane Writer's location: Washington, DCWriter's Military Unit: Union, Company C 6th RegimentAddressee: Friend Dick

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding battle of "First Bull Run" plus error printed envelope.Number of pages: 4 and envelope. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Chaplain Levi W. Norton Writer's location: Camp Caldwell, DEWriter's Military Unit: Union, 72 New York InfantryAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chaplain's letter describes services with sketch of tents and map.Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Charley Writer's location: Camp UnionWriter's Military Unit: Union, 1st Massachusetts CavalryAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "I go forth without fear of trembling"Number of pages: 3

did.unittitle: Writer: Cadmus M. Amoss Writer's location: Camp Washington, Yorktown, VAWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, Cobbs Georgia LegionAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Witnesses his first storm at sea - War has set him and thousands of others, ten years behind in business.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: A.A. Abernathy Writer's location: Bowling Green, KYWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, UnknownAddressee: Grandmother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "It is not my desire to kill any man"Number of pages: 1

did.unittitle: Writer: Francis W. Baker Writer's location: Falls Church, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Killed on their own soil by their own countrymen"Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Thomas Williams Writer's location: HQ 1st RegimentWriter's Military Unit: Union, 1st Regiment, New York CavalryAddressee: Col. McReynolds

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re: Charges against an officer for violent conduct.Number of pages: 3

did.unittitle: Writer: Charles H. Mendell Writer's location: Annapolis, MDWriter's Military Unit: Union, 23rd Regiment, Massachusetts VolunteersAddressee: My Kind Pastor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One of the hardest temptations is a game of cards.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: "C.C.N." Writer's location: Camp Benton, St. Louis, MOWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes life in the barracks - Makeshift Thanksgiving feast (Lithographed letterhead)Number of pages: 4. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: "A.K." Writer's location: Camp Barker, Cairo, ILWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Cousin Hattie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes scouting in Missouri - "Battles are not fought without the loss of life and some must fall"Number of pages: 8

did.unittitle: Writer: Capt. William B. Alexander Writer's location: Camp AndrewWriter's Military Unit: Union, 23rd Massachusetts InfantryAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Officer and slave talk sex.Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: 3rd Lt. William Nunnally Writer's location: Camp Ruffin, VAWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 13th NC InfantryAddressee: Brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mentions baseball, visit to CSS Merrimack, getting drunk on eggnog, kite flying.Number of pages: 2

did.unittitle: Writer: Mifflin Gilbert Writer's location: Camp GoodmanWriter's Military Unit: Union, 28th PA. Infantry VolunteersAddressee: Sister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hide bottle of brandy in loaf of bread"Number of pages: 2 1/2. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: E. Henry Writer's location: Camp Sorthumberland?Writer's Military Unit: Union, 96th PA InfantryAddressee: Father & Sisters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Balloon Reconnaissance over Manassas (See also Balloon 2/27/63 and 6/18/63).Number of pages: 5

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. George R. Baldwin Writer's location: Fort Scott, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, Co. C - 1st Connecticut, Heavy ArtilleryAddressee: Friend Ellery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Whore House Escapades.Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: J. W. Austin Writer's location: New Madrid, MOWriter's Military Unit: Union, 5th Iowa InfantryAddressee: Brother, Sister, Father

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Damaging storm, cannonating at Island #10, soldiers being killed and injured playing with shells.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: E.S. Reed Writer's location: City, NCWriter's Military Unit: Union, 23rd Massachusetts RegimentAddressee: Friend

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fighting in knee deep mud. "I got struck twice with a ball"Number of pages: 4. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Hank Lamereaux Writer's location: New Bern, NCWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Soldier struck by lightning.Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Capt. John N.Wiedemyer Writer's location: Camp Churchill Clark, Corinth, MSWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 6th Missouri InfantryAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Give one a patriot's grave but not a coward's name"Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Unsigned Writer's location: Camp No. 7, near Corinth, MSWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Soldiers prick their ears only when bulldogs (cannons) bark. Regiment issued new clothing.Number of pages: 4. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Capt. John M. Huntington Writer's location: Clarksburg, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 86th Regiment Ohio VolunteersAddressee: (Blank)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Whimsical Civil War BroadsideNumber of pages: 1

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Isaac Newton Writer's location: Ft Morgan, N&C RR, TNWriter's Military Unit: Union, 37th Illinois InfantryAddressee: Sister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Soldiers falling in love with locals - Trading salt and coffee for whiskey with rebels - deer hunt - (Original drawing on masthead) (See also Trading 6/25/63, 7/20/63, 8/10/64, 12/18/64).Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Col. J. A. Mulligan Writer's location: Camp Comiskey, New Creek, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 23rd Illinois VolunteersAddressee: Major Naughton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "It is sad to think that through all this quiet and magnificence ... there shall be blows and blood and ruin"Number of pages: 3

did.unittitle: Writer: Daniel Davis Writer's location: Near Culpepper C.H., VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 46th Pennsylvania VolunteersAddressee: Brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re: Counterfeit Confederate NotesNumber of pages: 3

did.unittitle: Writer: Major Clark S. Edwards Writer's location: HQ, Harrison Landing, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 5th Maine InfantryAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Midnight artillary battle - "Had a merry drunk"Number of pages: 5 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: C.S. Graham Writer's location: HeyworthWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Town buys a flag for its soldiers going off to war.Number of pages: 1 1/2

did.unittitle: Writer: Rebecca Wiswell Writer's location: Georgetown Seminary HospitalWriter's Military Unit: Union, NurseAddressee: Friend

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Union nurse writes of a soldier's death after Antietam (See also Nurse 9/29/63).Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Edward J. Rinebold Writer's location: VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 141st Pennsylvania VolunteersAddressee: Friend

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Soldier shoots himself in foot to try to get a discharge.Number of pages: 4 and envelope. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: E.T. Austin (sister of soldier) Writer's location: UnknownWriter's Military Unit: N/A, CivilianAddressee: Zadak Austin, 5th Battalion, NY Infantry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: How the brave soldier was wounded at Antietam.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Luther F. Davis Writer's location: Near Perryville, KYWriter's Military Unit: Union, Ohio 121st InfantryAddressee: Brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes "camp houses" and drillingNumber of pages: 3 and envelope. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Samuel F. Emerson Writer's location: Near Leesberg, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 3rd Maine InfantryAddressee: Father

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "I have already fought on seven different battlefields and I have seen enough"Number of pages: 2

did.unittitle: Writer: J.J. Campbell Writer's location: Brooke's Station, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 42nd Pennsylvania RegimentAddressee: Friend

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes foraging for food in a pig pen.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: C.R.P. Rogers Writer's location: Port RoyalWriter's Military Unit: Union, U.S.S. WabashAddressee: Son

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Father writes a moral code for his son.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Joseph H. Capen Writer's location: Camp Stevenson, NewbernWriter's Military Unit: Union, 44th Massachusetts InfantryAddressee: Parents and Brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lengthy description of all types of camp daily activities.Number of pages: 20 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Sgt. Lewis Sims Writer's location: Fort Pickeren near MemphisWriter's Military Unit: Union, 89th Indiana InfantryAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In a state of alarm, waiting to do battle with the rebels - mentions he is teaching a slave boy to read and write.Number of pages: 8

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Albert C. Pray Writer's location: Fort Schuyler, NYWriter's Military Unit: Union, 20th Maine InfantryAddressee: Brothers and Sister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comments on the war and on politicians and generals - Discusses forced march, Battle of South Mountain.Number of pages: 10 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Capt. Chief Quartermaster Charles G. Sawtelle Writer's location: Stafford Heights, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 2nd Corps. Army of PotomacAddressee: My dear Aurell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Officer apologizes for getting drunk (Name signed in artwork).Number of pages: 2

did.unittitle: Writer: J.C. Williams Writer's location: UnknownWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 7th South CarolinaAddressee: Parents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Snowball fight (See also Snowball 2/27/63).Number of pages: 2

did.unittitle: Writer: Lewis Writer's location: Near Murfreesboro, TNWriter's Military Unit: Union, 3rd Brigade 3rd Division 20th Army CorpsAddressee: Hettie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes a tour of the picket lines and the devastation around them - Plus slave story.Number of pages: 5 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: William S. Campbell Writer's location: Near Fredericksburg, VAWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 5th Alabama InfantryAddressee: Bill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Snowball fight. Yankee balloons - "Fight them to the last" .Number of pages: 6

did.unittitle: Writer: Capt. Watson Sloan Writer's location: Headquarters Picket Post No. 4 Clarksville, TNWriter's Military Unit: Union, 102nd Regiment OhioAddressee: Friends at Home

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts as a steamboat guard - using black labor for construction - a false alarm calls the regiment out to battle.Number of pages: 8 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: "M" (a woman) Writer's location: New Orleans, LAWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, UnknownAddressee: Friend

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Battle of the Parasols.Number of pages: 14

did.unittitle: Writer: Capt. John M. Weidemeyer Writer's location: Camp at Grand GulfWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 6th Missouri InfantryAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Love Letter .Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Sgt. J.E. Wallis Writer's location: Galveston, TXWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 20th Texas RegimentAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Battle of Big Brothel.Number of pages: 2 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Brother George Writer's location: Camp near Stafford C.H. VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 11th CorpsAddressee: Sister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lincoln reviews the troops - "He looks haggard and careworn". Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Nathan Hallock Writer's location: Near Falmouth, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 124th New York InfantryAddressee: Father

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The President looks very careworn" - Review of corps by Pres. Lincoln and Gen. Hooker Number of pages: 2

did.unittitle: Writer: Dennis Tuttle Writer's location: Camp Donna, Isabella, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A union corporal gives birth on the picket line.Number of pages: 4 1/2

did.unittitle: Writer: S.C. Wilkerson (?) Writer's location: Allisona, TNWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 18th Alabama RegimentAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A loving letter. Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Lt. Martin Miller Writer's location: McNairy Station, TNWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Sister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Crossing Great Bear Creek - Ladies chew and smoke tobacco and have very large feet!Number of pages: 3 1/4

did.unittitle: Writer: Corporal Roland J. Cook Writer's location: Bell Buckle, TNWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 2nd Arkansas InfantryAddressee: Cousin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses Negroes being carried off and conscripted by the Federals.Number of pages: 3

did.unittitle: Writer: H.R. Doud Writer's location: Camp at Banks FordWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Friend

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The rebs throwed shells at our balloon but did not hit it".Number of pages: 2 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: David M. Cushing Writer's location: Near Port HudsonWriter's Military Unit: Union, 53rd Massachusetts InfantryAddressee: Brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yanks and rebels sing - Flag of truce. Number of pages: 2 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: "Benby" Writer's location: Port HudsonWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Brother & Sister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fort Hudson surrenders-Rebels might have held on if they had not faced starvation.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Civil War Draft Notice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft notice sent to a resident of Salem, Massachusetts informing him that he is "legally drafted in the service of the United States". Rudin # M-62A

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. John Black Writer's location: Sharpsburg Ford, Potomac RiverWriter's Military Unit: Union, 12 Penn. CavalryAddressee: My loving Friend

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Union chats with Confederates and gives them whiskey after Battle of Gettysburg.Number of pages: 3

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 273

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Court martial of six soldiers who deserted and were sentenced to be shot. However, a compassionate Lincoln remitted all these sentences. Rudin # M-76B

did.unittitle: Writer: Herbert Writer's location: Union City, TNWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Mother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Excellent commentary on slavery and its ramifications.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Sgt. Sampson J.D. Whiteman Writer's location: Newbern, NCWriter's Military Unit: Union, 3rd New York CavalryAddressee: Cousin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All male cotillion- Advice on associating with friends.Number of pages: 8 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Edward Henry Writer's location: New Baltimore, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 96th Pennsylvania InfantryAddressee: Sister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drummed out of the Regiment.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Dr. Fossard Writer's location: Near Culpepper C.H., VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 146th Regiment New York VolunteersAddressee: Dan (?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Union surgeon and his lady of easy virtue.Number of pages: 3

did.unittitle: Writer: Phebe J. Parker Writer's location: Washington, DCWriter's Military Unit: Union, NurseAddressee: Brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Civil War Nurse discusses nursing activities, touring Washington and seeing Pres. Lincoln (See also Nurse 9/20/62).Number of pages: 2 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Capt. A.W. Alvord Writer's location: Camp Austin, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 109th New York VolunteersAddressee: Adjutant Jewett

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pleading for Whiskey-with response by Jewett on verso.Number of pages: 1

did.unittitle: Writer: James W. Bartlett Writer's location: Blain's Cross Roads, TNWriter's Military Unit: Union, 35th Massachusetts InfantryAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First use of barbed wire-soldiers wearing all kinds of clothing, including women's.Number of pages: 6

did.unittitle: Writer: Qtrmaster J.F. Andrews Writer's location: Washington, DCWriter's Military Unit: Union, 1st Rhode Island CavalryAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lincoln's New Year's Reception.Number of pages: 4 and illustrated envelope. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Lewis Josselyn Writer's location: Baton Rouge, LAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 38th Massachusetts InfantryAddressee: Mother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Going to a "nigger ball"Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Corp. Arthur M. Stone Writer's location: Cumberland, MDWriter's Military Unit: Union, 34th Massachusetts InfantryAddressee: Mother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drunk soldier claims he was captured by the ConfederatesNumber of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Col. Charles W. Drew Writer's location: Port HudsonWriter's Military Unit: Union, 4th Infantry Corps d'AfriqueAddressee: Col. Charles Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hanky Panky in the Corps d'AfriqueNumber of pages: 6 1/4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Benjamin M. Florence Writer's location: UnknownWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 17th Virginia InfantryAddressee: Miss Ellen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Love letter home.Number of pages: 2

did.unittitle: Writer: A.A. Winn Writer's location: Madison Station, VAWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, UnknownAddressee: Father

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fighting sham battle-Plus second brief undated letter.Number of pages: 6

did.unittitle: Writer: Capt. Charles R. Taylor Writer's location: Grove Church, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 2nd Pennsylvania CavalryAddressee: Col. Taylor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pertaining to woman coming into lines with information on CSA troops in the area.Number of pages: 1

did.unittitle: Writer: Peleg Bradford Writer's location: Fort Sumner, MDWriter's Military Unit: Union, 1st Maine ArtillaryAddressee: Dearest Friend

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Love letter to "Cynth" McPherson (See also Love Letters 3/27/63, 5/7/63, 3/18/64, 7/2/64, 7/27/64, 10/26/64, 10/27/64).Number of pages: 2 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. John M. Lovejoy Writer's location: Near Spotsylvania Court House, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 121st New York InfantryAddressee: Mother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Battle of Spotsylvania - " a ball came so near to my head that I was knocked down by the concussion of air"Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Henry Webb Writer's location: Point Lookout Prison, MDWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 50th Virginia InfantryAddressee: Mrs. Smiley

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two remarkable letters written to the mother of a soldier Webb killed at the Battle of the Wilderness - Written on pages taken from a book.Number of pages: 2

did.unittitle: Writer: Lewis Hanback Writer's location: Camp on Good Hope BattlefieldWriter's Military Unit: Union, 27th Illinois InfantryAddressee: Darling Hettie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Confederate soldier jumps into Yankee rifle pits - "How are you Yanks?"Number of pages: 3 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Major Thomas Norris Writer's location: In the field near Dallas, GAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 111th Ohio VolunteersAddressee: Mother (Rebecca Norris)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recounts the horrors of war and the tragic death of his brother (James Norris).Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Capt. Frederick B. Doten Writer's location: Hdqtrs Third BrigadeWriter's Military Unit: Union, 14th Connecticut InfantryAddressee: Darling Georgie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First two pages love letters to Georgie Welles. Picket firing ceases by mutual consent so that both sides can cook their cakes and coffee. Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: C.C. Mobley Writer's location: Division Hospital near PetersburgWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 43rd Alabama InfantryAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written on Union Morning Report Form - Prepared to meet death and God - Mails delayed - Paid 50 cents for this sheet of paper and much more.Number of pages: 2. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Thomas Jefferson Campbell (C ) Writer's location: Near Berryville, VAWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 48th Virginia InfantryAddressee: Cousin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written on captured Union song sheet - has marched over 500 miles - unit has barely enough men for a color guard.Number of pages: 2 and envelope. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Unknown Soldier - Job Writer's location: Triana, ALWriter's Military Unit: Unknown Addressee: Flora

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Refers to "I don't have any inclination whatever of dying" - Talks about "What a fine thing letter writing is" - Mentions his loving attachment to Flora.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Nason B. Will Writer's location: Camp 12th VA, Sussex County, VAWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 12th Virginia CavalryAddressee: Cousin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Women can fight with broomsticks, flat irons, butcher knives, etc.Number of pages: 2 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: George B. Noyes Writer's location: Deep Bottom, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 11th Maine InfantryAddressee: Friend Emery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The regiment that never runs. "Honor lies on the field and disgrace in the gutter"Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Joseph Henry Pierce Writer's location: Before PetersburgWriter's Military Unit: Union, 36th Massachusetts InfantryAddressee: Friend

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many of the officers were known to be drunk at the Battle of Crater.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Ezra P. Prentice Writer's location: Camp near PetersburgWriter's Military Unit: Union, 5th New York Veteran Volunteers InfantryAddressee: Mother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Life on the picket lines-Trading with the Rebels. Number of pages: 3

did.unittitle: Writer: Parris M Lauw Writer's location: Berryville, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 18th Connecticut InfantryAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pertaining to the guerrillas operating in the area and tactics against them.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Ensign J. Louis Harris Writer's location: Off Fort Morgan, Mobile BayWriter's Military Unit: Union, U.S.S. ChickasawAddressee: Dear Emmie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fighting on board the ironclad "Chickasaw".Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: F. H. Bond Writer's location: Fort Federal Hill Baltimore, MDWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Mother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Brown-Rebel prisoners-Other war content.Number of pages: 4. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Asst Surgeon C. James Terrell Writer's location: East View?Writer's Military Unit: Confederate, UnknownAddressee: General William Taliaferro

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prays for victory and the relief of POWs - Mentions many taking the oath of allegiance to the U.S.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Edward H. Daugherty Writer's location: Pine Bluff, ARWriter's Military Unit: Union, 28th WisconsinAddressee: Brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fighting rebels in Arkansas and hanging a Confederate spy.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Matthew McCann Writer's location: Camp before PetersburgWriter's Military Unit: Union, 132nd RegimentAddressee: Wife and Child

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fraternization - Pickets negotiate a cease fire at the front.Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: N. Palmer Writer's location: Martinsburg, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Parents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Description of camp life prior to battle. Number of pages: 3. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: "ALR" Writer's location: Fort Hayes, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Patriotic letter - "In order to preserve this union unsullied and untarnished, slavery must become extinct"Number of pages: 3. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Robert Taylor Writer's location: Camp Casey Washington DCWriter's Military Unit: Union, 45th Pennsylvania Regiment Co. BAddressee: Miss Rachel Ziza

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Love Letter comments, see letter below from 10/27/1864 - 1. Seems that Haws wrote the letter for Taylor. 2. Both letters seem to be written in the same hand. 3. Both women live in Yelaington, Davis County, KY. 4. Both men are in the 45th PA Regiment. 5. Poetry is the same in both letters. Number of pages: 3 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Thorton Haws Writer's location: Camp Casey Washington DCWriter's Military Unit: Union, 45th Pennsylvania Regiment Co. BAddressee: Miss Pegga Taylor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Love Letter comments, see letter above from 10/26/1864 - 1. Seems that Haws wrote the letter for Taylor. 2. Both letters seem to be written in the same hand. 3. Both women live in Yelaington, Davis County, KY. 4. Both men are in the 45th PA Regiment. 5. Poetry is the same in both letters. Number of pages: 3 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. James E. Russell Writer's location: Point Lookout, MDWriter's Military Unit: Union, 5th Massachusetts CavalryAddressee: Friend Mr. Okson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Colored cavalryman warns the rebels that the black Yankees are coming.Number of pages: 4. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Edward Minnie (?) Writer's location: Before Petersburg, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Miss. Mary Marsh

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Trading sugar and coffee for tobacco - also trading hats for rebel caps.Number of pages: 3 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: 2nd Lt. Charles W. Smith Writer's location: Near Petersbury, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 1st Connecticut ArtilleryAddressee: Miss Emma Leach

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yankee deserts to the rebels.Number of pages: 3 1/2 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Capt. S. Boyer Davis Writer's location: Johnson's Island, OHWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, UnknownAddressee: My Dear Friends

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: POW letter written the night before Davis was to be hanged as a spy.Number of pages: 1

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. E.A. Klipstein Writer's location: Camp 8th VA RegimentWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 8th Virginia InfantryAddressee: Son James

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Goose shoot between Yankee and Confederate soldiers.Number of pages: 2

did.unittitle: Writer: James Henderson Writer's location: Camp 67th, OHWriter's Military Unit: Union, 67th Ohio InfantryAddressee: Brother and Sister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exchange papers with rebels - Rebels keep constant firing at night to prevent desertions but they still occur.Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: William H. Green Writer's location: Nashville, TNWriter's Military Unit: Union, 45th New York VolunteersAddressee: Father and Mother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tenessee women spit tobacco and smoke cigars - 14 year old deserter.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: H.D. Burr Writer's location: On the Weldon RoadWriter's Military Unit: Union, 2nd Connecticut ArtilleryAddressee: Sister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "What an ocean of blood it has taken to wipe out the stain of slavery"Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Charles B. Church Writer's location: Camp near Petersburg, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 122nd Ohio InfantryAddressee: Parents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Description of an execution.Number of pages: 3 and envelope. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Samuel L. Graham Writer's location: Aunt Fanny Daniel's HouseWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 34th Virginia InfantryAddressee: Cousins

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Evacuation and fall of Richmond.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Charles L. Fales Writer's location: Near Clover Hill (Appomattox 30 miles from Lynchburg, VA)Writer's Military Unit: Union, 11th Massachusetts. VolunteersAddressee: Sister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lee's surrender.Number of pages: 4. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Col. William McArthur Writer's location: Near Appomattox Courthouse in the field, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 8th Maine VolunteersAddressee: Father

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lee's surrender.Number of pages: 3 1/2

did.unittitle: Writer: Corp. Patrick Murphy Writer's location: Camp near Burksville, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, UnknownAddressee: Uncle and Aunt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lee's Surrender and Lincoln's Assassination.Number of pages: 3. Illustrated.

did.unittitle: Writer: Capt. Samuel C. Pierce Writer's location: Suffolk, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 3rd New York CavalryAddressee: Dearest Ellen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lincoln's Assassination.Number of pages: 8

did.unittitle: Writer: Corp. Arthur M. Stone Writer's location: Richmond, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 34th Massachusetts InfantryAddressee: Mother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Surrender of Johnston - Assassination of Lincoln - Appomattox captured flags sent to Washington, DC.Number of pages: 4 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: L.A. Jobe Writer's location: Gainsville, ALWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, 9th Mississippi CavalryAddressee: Dearest Kate

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Surrender of General Canby and General Johnston.Number of pages: 3 1/2

did.unittitle: Writer: Col. William McArthur Writer's location: Near Richmond, VAWriter's Military Unit: Union, 8th Maine VolunteersAddressee: Father

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: War's End.Number of pages: 2

did.unittitle: Writer: John Allen Writer's location: Hoffman House, NYWriter's Military Unit: N/A, CivilianAddressee: Nephew

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "What an eventful month has been the last in the history of the nation" - mentions surrender of Lee and assassination of Lincoln.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: Thomas J. Moore Writer's location: U.S. Military Prison Johnson's Island, OHWriter's Military Unit: Confederate, UnknownAddressee: Cousin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Confederate prisoner hates to take The Oath of Allegiance.Number of pages: 1

did.unittitle: Writer: Pvt. Charles W. Wall Writer's location: Washington, DCWriter's Military Unit: Union, New York VolunteersAddressee: Capt. William Morgan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Requests leniency for having insulted the captain while drunk.Number of pages: 2 1/2

did.unittitle: Writer: Frank Writer's location: Brooklyn, NYWriter's Military Unit: N/A, Probably a CivilianAddressee: Mrs. Morehouse

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "If anyone has wrongs to correct, the ballot box is the place to do it and not the battlefield"Number of pages: 3 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Series II. Letters from Dr. William Harrison Githens

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dr. Githens wrote most of these letters while serving as a surgeon with the 78th Regiment, Illinois Volunteers, 2nd Brigade, 2nd Division 14th Army Corps, and the U.S. General Hospital Division No. 10.

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Camp near Hamburg, TNAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Still in all this beautiful land is desolation -the desolation of war." "Our boys seem determined - and most of them are anxious to fight."Number of pages: 3

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Philadelphia, PA Addressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Studying medicine in Phil.-Tours the city during Thanksgiving (Letter is incomplete).Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Bridgeport, ALAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes crossing the mountains on a hard seven mile march with ambulances and twelve hundred teams in the ambulance train.Number of pages: 3

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Field hospital three miles north of Chattanooga, TNAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Terrible battle on Sunday-"we had 90 ambulances and about 40 wagons loaded-estimated at 700 wounded some of them very badly." "Sixty miles over the mountains"..."six days going and coming"-"thousands of wounded here in tents"..."our boys have proved themselves brave and noble soldiers".Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Chattanooga, TNAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Deserters still come in large numbers than ever. They say there is no use in fighting us any longer that the country is alive with "Yanks"..."It requires a hard struggle to decide between one's family and his country in danger"..."the hardships that may follow are almost too much to be borne. Yet in after years those who live to see the end will look back with pride" - Discusses his finances.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Camp near Chattanooga, TNAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Back from almost a month long march - Describes the conditions on their return. "Our poor boys were nearly naked-dirty and barefooted". Discusses a possible leave of absence. Shows concern for his wife's pregnancy (confinement). Reviews his finances. "I will have the proud satisfaction of having done my duty for my country."Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Hospital, Chattanooga, TNAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wife is about to give birth and Githens is anxious to know the result-Talks about a Surgeon's position and the possibility of going home-Mentions a sudden storm and very cold weather-Discusses use of churches and fine residences for hospitals, officer headquarters and for defenses - "This country will bear the devastating marks of war for many a year".Number of pages: 3

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Near Chattanooga, TNAddressee: Louise (Daughter)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reminds her of the importance of writing and spelling, as well as music - Wants her to be a good scholar -""Jack" (a black boy) has just come with his book wrong end up to ask me what some little letters spell"..."He seems pretty sharp"..."some of the Black Boys learn to read in a few weeks"... "if they only had teachers some of them would make good scholars"... "you wish for me to come home - how will the poor wounded boys get along - they must be taken care of"..."some of the poor fellows still lay here in the field Hospitals - poor and pale" - Talks about family affairs. Signed "Father"Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: U.S. General Hospital, Chattanooga, TNAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wife has given birth to a boy-Mentions children and family-Discusses a leave of absence disapproved-Conditions at camp are much improved-Mentions Battle of Chickamauga and Mission Ridge-Many people are returning, bands are playng as civilization returns.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: U.S. General Hopsital, Chattanooga, TNAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Talks of church services, the singing, the sermon-Anxious to get home on leave of absence-Thinks, for the children, "I could commence with new energies" and "make a name for myself that they would be proud of". Closes by saying "I have seen enough of battles and war-and have satisfied myself that I am not a coward-but a man is a fool to want to see more than one battle". (See also 9/18/64).Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Brigade hospital, near Rossville, GAAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: He is very homesick and has received no letters- But "when I think how much is needed and how much willing hands may do, I feel that there is no way but to gather fresh courage and try a while longer" - Talks about deserters - "The desolation and sorrow that they have caused will be a stain on them as long as they live"..."poor deluded mortals they know not what they did".Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Headquarters, near Rossville, GAAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May at any moment get marching orders and is sending most of his clothing to the rear-Went out to the battlefields to take up the dead for burial in the new U.S. Cemetery in Chattanooga-"we were glad to have a chance to put the poor fellows where they can rest in peace-we must not think defeat after having gained so much". Also talks about their financial position.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Camp, in the woods in the rail road about five miles from Marietta, GAAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses a potential battle for Marietta and is very positive about their military position-Mentions the Southern corn diet-Wishes he could be home for the 4th of July-Describes the band music that cheers the troops-Discusses the rebel deserters who are lied to by their officers and keep coming every day-"the poor deluded folks keep on fighting and sacrificing thousands of lives on both sides" - Army has complete confidence in General Sherman and "we'll go wherever he tells them".Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Camp, three or four miles southwest of Marietta, GAAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writes about Church - "today being a beautiful Sabbath as calm and quiet as though no such thing as war existed" - Wishes he was home - Mentions General Sherman's strategy of "running around their (Rebel) ends" - Discusses his pay and his finances.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Hospital, Chattahoochee River, GAAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mentions "there has been but little fighting for a few days...we have a very large force across the river and the prospect is very fair that we will be in Atlanta before long" - Writes "ours and the Rebel picket lines are close together...will all have some wonderful old soldier stories to tell if we get home".Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Hospital, Chattahoochie River, Trinings (?) Station, GAAddressee: Daughter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writes "Don't think how anxious we soldiers are to get letters on anything from home - reminding us of comfort and plenty-and the quiet times of peace" - Asks that his daughter attend diligently to her studies - Talks about his son Willie - Hopes Ma will have enough money to raise chickens and two or three pigs - Mentions that the sick and wounded get plenty to eat and are well cared for.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Hospital, 4 Miles from the Chattahoochee on the road to Atlanta, GAAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Atlanta is ours" - "We had quite a number wounded in the Division and a good many sick"..." dressing wounded before sunrise-and worked til late in the day before ambulances were sent - then we loaded and sent away load after load until late in the night"..."I don't doubt that there is more rejoicing at home than here" - "the soldier soon becomes indifferent to all joys and sorrows" - Describes John McGovern's roadside grave - "How we long for the slaughter to stop"Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Hospital, 5 or 6 miles from Atlanta, GAAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mentions that he was mistaken; Atlanta has not been taken but their forces occupy strong positions-Writes about possible new hospital locations-Conditions are good except for thousands of flies - "They are into everything-we hardly bother to skim them out of our coffee or soup." Talks about how hard it is to replace clothing - "Things are so enormously high that I can hardly afford them" (Letter is incomplete)Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Hospital, 5 miles S.W. Atlanta, GAAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Talks about the children, their finances, improvements on the house - He needs clothes -"I am the shabbiest dressed Medical Officer here" - Comments on the campaign "our Commander is not very keen to take Atlanta just at present" - Discusses in graphic terms the treatment of battle wounds.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Hospital, Atlanta, GAAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mentions improvements to the house-His division is going to garrison Atlanta-He is in charge of the Amputated Ward and the worst cases and gets a great many compliments from the soldiers- Gen'l Sherman has issued orders to remove the rebel civilians-"it seems cruel almost to require families to leave their homes and go, they know not where" - Discusses distinctions in rank -Talks about a future medical practice and that he will no longer accept any position in the Army.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Dept. Cumberland Medical College Atlanta, GAAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mentions slowly entering Atlanta-Using bales of cotton for beds-"I suppose there was a good deal of rejoicing all through the North at our success-but we took it very cooly-one thing made it different, our sad losses-and the care of the wounded". Describes the environment, train whistles, engine bells, various bands, etc. - "quite a contrast to laying out in the woods for months and being treated to ten inch shells for music" - Balance of letter is about getting paid and his finances; also about the surgical skills he has developed.Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Medical College, Atlanta, GAAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses a church service - Only thing missing is the female voice - Soldiers on guard at the door - Soldiers are taking "a resting spell" - Working day and night to build comfortable quarters - Mentions how homes are destroyed to get wood and brick. Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Officers' Hospital, 24th and Chestnut St. Philadelphia, PAAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "I feel like getting well rapidly-gaining strength quite fast"..."I expect it will be several days yet before I am in condition to travel". Talks about getting home and his plans for the future - "I shall count the hours until I am free once more yet I would much prefer to see the war ended first-You know I would get a little credit for the long years of hardship already endured if I should come home a few months before the rest did. Still that would matter very little if my own conscience was satisfied" Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Camp, Holly Springs, NCAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "This cruel war is over at last and we are to start for home" - Talks about travel arrangements to get home - Has ordered newspapers containing information about Lincoln's murder - Closes letter by saying "when orders are read that all the rebel armies have surrendered and that we are to go home, then the woods ring - old hats go up into the air and every kind of noisy demonstration is resorted to"Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Headquarters, near Fort Slemmer, Washington, DCAddressee: Daughter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: He is on his way home hopefully looking forward to a 4th of July welcome celebration - "all these days of toil are over and I hope forever - still we do not regret what we have endured when we think how much good it has done"Number of pages: 2

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Headquarters, camp, near Fort Slemmer, Washington , DC Addressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Disappointed that he has been detained so long and is going through such tedious paperwork - "I shall be so glad when I am once more free - when I can call myself and my time my own"..."I am more impatient at a week's delay than I used to be at a month"..."it seems that I have grown so accustomed to excitement and danger that the dull monotony of home life will soon tire - I hope not - for I used to love home as well as anyone"Number of pages: 4

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Hamilton, ILAddressee: Sarah (Githen's grand - daughter)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Congratulates his granddaughter on the fact that she is about to receive her teacher's credentials - Talks about the weather and the and the local bird population - Mentions family affairs - Signed "Gramps"Number of pages: 3 and envelope.

did.unittitle: Writer: The Carthage Republican Writer's location: Carthage, IL

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Carthage Republican - Githens' Obituary

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: UnknownAddressee: Mr. President

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses horticulture and gardening.Number of pages: 8 page photocopy

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Nashiville, Tennessee and Macon City, Missouri Addressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three letters, two are just dealer description not the actual letter, all are copies. Discuss churches in Nashville TN, illness of soldiers, living conditions, Battle of Platt City, MO.Number of pages: 5 pages of photocopies

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: VariousAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four letters, two are just dealer description, not the actual letter, all are copies. Discuss Fort Payne, Kentucky, Bird's Point Missouri, drunkenness, buying a horse, Pittsburgh Landing, Tennessee River, Cairo, illness of men.Number of pages: 8 pages of photocopies

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: Philadelphia, PA Addressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six letters, all are copies. Discuss taking musket balls from trees as keepsakes, Continental Hotel in Philadelphia Pennsylvania, Cousin Sammy Scull, taking classes in Philadelphia and wondering about being able to afford a graduating, visiting the mint to see money being made and the Academy of Sciences, also other sites of Philadelphia including the Liberty Bell and Independence Hall. Mentions church attendees in their furs, wishing he and his wife were rich to travel, wanting to be home for Christmas.Number of pages: 16 pages of photocopies

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: VariousAddressee: Wife and daughter Louise

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nine letters, several are just dealer description not the actual letter, all are copies. Discuss troop movements, homesickness, battle at Franklin Tennessee, botched hanging of Confederate spies, taking over a confederate camp, with map, encouragement to daughter to keep up studies,Number of pages: 17 pages of photocopies

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: VariousAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six letters, all are copies. Discuss wounded soldiers, hunger, measles, mumps, smallpox, blistered feet, Confederate deserters, Chattanooga Tennessee, Chickamauga Station battle, sketches of soldier life.Number of pages:21 pages of photocopies

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: VariousAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seven letters, several are just dealer description not the actual letter, all are copies. Discuss a brother digging grave for another brother, re-electing Lincoln, citizen rations, Georgia, bringing decaying remains in from the battlefield, church.Number of pages: 17 pages of photocopies

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: VariousAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five letters, several are just dealer description not the actual letter, all are copies. Discuss Chattanooga, starving rebels, buying a house, bad vaccines causing the soldiers sores, retrieving dead from the field.Number of pages:18 pages of photocopies

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: VariousAddressee: Wife and daughter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nine letters, several are just dealer description not the actual letter, all are copies. Discuss capturing several thousand pounds of tobacco and prisoners, amputations, women, battle of Kennisaw Mountain, schoolwork of his daughter, Atlanta,Number of pages: 16 pages of photocopies

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: VariousAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five letters, all are copies. Discuss taking in rebel wounded and dead, severe wounds, maggots in wounds, shot to the head of Colonel, Van Vleck, rebels destroying millions of dollars of property as they retreated, people of the South relating to Yankees,Number of pages: 23 pages of photocopies

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: VariousAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six letters, all are copies. Discuss cold weather sleeping, soldier life, lack of pay, occupying a wealthy plantation mansion, U.S. Christian Commission, Christmas.Number of pages: 12 pages of photocopies

did.unittitle: Writer: William Harrison Githens Writer's location: VariousAddressee: Wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nine letters, all are copies. Discuss camp life, battles, capture of Richmond and Petersburg, end of the war,Number of pages: 23 pages of photocopies

did.unittitle: Series III. Documents
Draft avoidance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Commutation Money receipt for avoiding the Union draft by paying a $300 fee. Yonkers, NY. Rudin # M-60

did.unittitle: Draft avoidance: certificate of substitution

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Form used when one person paid another to take their place in the Military Service during the Civil War. Newton Green pays Asathus A. Hart $400 to act as his substitute. Rudin # M-61

did.unittitle: Draft avoidance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Certificate certifies that H.C. Gregerson is entitled to be paid $300 as a substitute for a drafted NY Policeman. Additional document acknowledges receipt of the $300. Rudin # M-62

did.unittitle: Substitute soldier bounty payment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Certificate names a substitute soldier with a bounty payment of $300. Additional document releases NY Substitute Committee of claim. Rudin # M-63

did.unittitle: Substitute broker card

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Business card for Haynes, Webb and Co. General Substitute Brokers - Bounties paid to Substitutes and Volunteers to fight in Civil War. Rudin # M-63A

did.unittitle: New York Draft Exemptions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Commutation Money Receipt indicating commutation money received from 18 men exempting them from the Federal Draft. Rudin # M-64

did.unittitle: Drummer boy discharge

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Certificate of Discharge for a 13 year old drummer boy by reason of a physical disability. Rudin # M-65

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Act to provide for the appointment of a General in Chief of the Armies of the Confederate States. Rudin # A-139

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accused of desertion, Pvt. George Hoffer receives a sentence of 10 years at hard labor with a ball of 24 pounds attached to his leg by a chain four feet long. Rudin # M-73A

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 24

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The restoration of the Union flag at Fort Sumter. Rudin # A-13B

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 32

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Surgeon dismissed from the service for violation of 39th Article of War. (Fraudulent accounts) Rudin # M-73

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 43

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Court martial of Major McKinstry, Quartermaster, charged with neglect of duty (buying in collusion with others) in 61 specifications. Lincoln approved sentence of dismissal from the service. Inked signature of General Hiram Scofield. Rudin # M-72

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 53

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Court martials of deserters - men ordered to have their heads shaved and branded with the letter "D" and one man to be shot to death. Rudin # M-76

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 147

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Lincoln changes sentences of three soldiers. Rudin # M-67

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 148

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lincoln approves sentence of dishonorable discharge for a soldier who pleaded guilty to stealing from another private. Rudin # M-71A

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 149

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Court martial of soldier for desertion and assault with intent to commit robbery and murder. Lincoln approves sentence to be shot. Rudin # M-76A

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 190

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pay schedule for men enlisting in the Regular Army offering premium, advanced pay, and bounty. Rudin # M-66

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 209

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Court martial of a soldier found guilty of drunkenness on duty and conduct unbecoming an officer. Rudin # M-68

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 230

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Court Martial found four men guilty of desertion, violation of oath of allegiance, and murder. Lincoln showed compassion and remitted all sentences. Two copies. Rudin # M-70, 75

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 240

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Court Martial of two soldiers found guilty of desertion. Rudin # M-69

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 267

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General Orders accusing eight different men of various charges punishable by a sentence of "To be shot to death". Rudin # M-74

did.unittitle: General Orders No. 306

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lincoln approves sentence of hanging for a soldier accused of being a guerilla. Rudin # M-71

did.unittitle: Engraving of the naval engagement between the Merrimac and the Monitor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rudin # M-77

did.unittitle: Fort Sumter Note

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Twenty-five cent note - The Bank of the State of South Carolina - Fort Sumter on fire. Rudin # A-13A

did.unittitle: Bounty: "Statement of Volunteer"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Soldier receives a $300 bounty for enlisting. Rudin # M-41

did.unittitle: Bounty Payment Bond

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Payment Bond for $100 "on the call by the President for 500,000 men". To James Payne. Rudin # M-42

did.unittitle: Bounty Receipt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: County Bounty Receipt for $6000 for fifteen union soldiers who signed up for the Civil War for three years. Rudin # M-44

did.unittitle: Bounty Receipt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bounty receipt for $800 for two union soldiers who signed up for the Civil War for three years. Rudin # M-45

did.unittitle: Bounty Fund Bond

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bond used to raise funds to pay bounties to volunteers. ($300 bounty fund, interest 6%). Rudin # M-45AA

did.unittitle: Civil War Recruiting Broadside

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enlistment Recruiting Broadside for Mass. 2nd Regiment Heavy Artillery. Rudin # M-55

did.unittitle: Confederate Circular

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Circular regarding officer's behavior in the field, by order of Brigadier General P.O. Hebert. Rudin # M-55A

did.unittitle: Wallpaper Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Confederate "Chaudron's Spelling Book" - Front and back covers printed on double-folded, ornate fancy wallpaper (see inside back cover and also "To the Public" preface referring to "scarcity of materials".) From the Henderschott Collection of Confederate Imprints. Rudin # M-97

did.unittitle: Wallpaper envelope plus letter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wallpaper envelope (no stamp) with letter from Sgt. Baker hand-carried to wife regarding clothing and naming of their baby. Rudin # M-98

did.unittitle: Necessity Paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Being short of paper, soldier, Elisha O. Drake, writes on Soldier's Eagle Discharge Form. Discusses paying bills, re-enlisting, and photographs of Generals. Rudin # M-101

did.unittitle: Necessity Paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Albert R. Whitney writes letter on Magnus hand colored patriotic song sheet discussing the campground, the lack of water, and the need for a box of clothing. Rudin # M-101A

did.unittitle: Necessity Paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: CSA North Carolina 25 Cent Note printed on back of 1863 North Carolina $20 Note. Rudin # M-101B

did.unittitle: Necessity Paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five Confederate Georgia Treasury Notes in denominations of 5 cents, 10 cents, 25 cents, 50 cents, and 75 cents, all printed on reverse of $4 note (not a popular denomination) because of the scarcity of paper. Rudin # M-101C

did.unittitle: Necessity Stationary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Confederate Patriotic Stationery with cannon, flag, and patriotic verse printed on brown Confederate necessity paper. Two different size letter sheets unused plus one envelope. Rudin # M-83

did.unittitle: Necessity Paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter written by Union soldier to his sister on captured Confederate post office ledger. Rudin # M-83A

did.unittitle: Necessity Paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement for a deceased Confederate soldier on necessity paper printed on official Republic of Mexico paper. Rudin # M-84

did.unittitle: Necessity Paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hand made envelope from a printed sheet of a piano songbook. Postally used. Capt. HL Van Eaton. Rudin # M-85

did.unittitle: Necessity Paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Confederate Soldiers' Pass printed on verso of a Mississippi government form. Rudin # M-86A

did.unittitle: Necessity Paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Soldier's letter (T.A. Taggart) on mocha colored necessity paper. Rudin # M-86B

did.unittitle: Wallpaper newspaper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Junior Register" - Rare wallpaper edition printed on the back of wallpaper due to shortage of newsprint. Rudin # M-100

did.unittitle: Necessity paper newspaper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Houston Daily Telegraph printed on tan corn husk paper. Rudin # M-100A

did.unittitle: Wallpaper newspaper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Tri-Weekly Telegraph printed on green corn husk paper. Rudin # M-100B

did.unittitle: Civil War Pass

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Civil War Pass allowing "F. Pool 3 days over the Bridges within the Original lines" signed by Captain Drake De Kay. Rudin # M-87

did.unittitle: Password (confederate)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An original password paper signed by Phinney Adjt. Rudin # M-88

did.unittitle: Davis, Jefferson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Capture of Jeff Davis lithograph. Rudin # A-59

did.unittitle: Davis, Jefferson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Insightful letter into the thinking of Jefferson Davis. Rudin # A-60

did.unittitle: Program for the second Inauguration of Jefferson Davis and Alexander Stephens

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rudin # A-58

did.unittitle: Davis, Jefferson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rudin # A-57

did.unittitle: Miniature of Lee's General Orders No. 9

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lee's Farewell Address in a 1" square in the top left corner with hand-colored flags on a miniature cover. Written by Edward Pollard. Rudin # M-78

did.unittitle: "Dix" Note

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Famous Dix Note that caused the South to be called "Dixie". Rudin # M-59
